observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, to ensure accuracy, verifiability, and proper presentation of the accounts under Infrastructure Assets:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all PPEs under the Infrastructure Asset Accounts and reconcile the same with the recorded PPE in the books;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record infrastructure assets constructed by the LGU before 2015 and ensure that depreciation expenses for Infrastructure Assets are recognized annually; and
Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit in a prescribed format a complete and accurate report of RPCPPE for the current year to the Municipal Accountant and the COA before January 31 of the succeeding year.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Establish a control system that would strictly monitor the collections and remittances of all collectors and ensure that all collections are promptly remitted and deposited intact; and
Cause the stoppage of incurrence of recurring /habitual delayed remittances of collectors and furnish the Audit Team of the action taken thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and/or Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the duplicate copies together with the RCDs of the aforesaid unaccounted official receipts and validated deposit slips. Pending submission, the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Audit Team the triplicate copies of unaccounted official receipts, copy of RCDs, and validated deposit slips for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Require the Custodian of accountable forms to refrain from issuing stubs of official receipts without verification whether previously issued stub was already consumed, handed over and properly accounted for; and
Ensure that Accountable Forms/Official Receipts in the possession of Collecting Officers do not exceed the actual needs or use.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer take custody of the accountable forms, maintain an updated stock card and monitor the balance on hand, approximate average consumption, avoid overstocking, and safeguard against loss or misuse. When deciding to purchase, ensure that the quantity should not exceed three months consumption or one year at the maximum. In addition, the prescribed Requisition and Issue Slips should be utilized for issuances of accountable forms to the collectors.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Discontinue granting cash advance when the utility of which cannot be ascertained at a speciﬁc time; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances are already liquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer include the checks in his individual RAAF and, accordingly, in the consolidated RAAF and ensure preparation and submission of these RAAFs according to the cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the status of transferred funds to identify unutilized balances of completed PPAs, and comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 on the return of the unexpended balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Submit all lacking documentary requirements; and
Strictly follow the provisions of the MOA as such record the transaction to appropriate account, in this case as Due from NGOs/POs instead of Donations.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:
Ensure that the medical device product, particularly, COVID-19 test kit to be procured, is included in the List of the Approved Antibody Rapid Test Kits for Commercial Use, has been certified by the FDA, and has Special Certification; and
Regularly check and adhere to FDA issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Committee on Daet Scholarship Program to:
Adopt a well-documented procurement process of services in accordance with the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (R-IRR) of RA No. 9184.
Ensure that every Driving NC II scholar undergo competency assessment from TESDA by including it in the program design or ordinance and require documentation thereto;
Strictly adhere to the provisions of Daet Municipal Ordinance No. 327-2018 as to allowable courses for the scholarship program as well as the eligibility of the applicants; and
Limit the grant of scholarships only to poor and deserving students of the Municipality of Daet in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 7160.

the BAC to submit promptly all contracts
with their supporting documents to the
Audit Team for review within five days from
perfection thereof in compliance with
existing regulations. Likewise, request for
technical inspection for completed projects
must be submitted to the Commission on
Audit. Any unjustified failure of the
officials and employees concerned to
comply with the requirements herein
imposed shall be subject to the
administrative disciplinary action provided
in (a) Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445; (b)
Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised
Administrative Code of 1987; and (c)
Section 11 of R.A. No. 6713.
observations.managementAction:
Some contracts with
their supporting
documents were
submitted beyond the
prescribed period.
observations.reason:
This observation and
recommendation was
reiterated in AAR for CY
2016.

observations.recommendation:
‘We recommended that Management strictly
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 82-186 and 75-6 dated May 3, 1982 and
November 7, 1975, respectively, to wit:
o Register and secure a government plate
for the vehicles;

e Mark the vehicles “FOR OFFICIAL
USE ONLY” together with the name
and logo of the Municipality;

e Direct the designated officer to pay the
corresponding  penalties  of  the
unregistered vehicles; and

e Stop charging gasoline and other
expenses for unregistered vehicles until
compliance to the provisions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
e Municipal Accountant should see to it
that complete supporting documents were
complied with before approval of claims;

e General Services Office (GSO) should
maintain a detailed record of the repairs
made on all motor vehicles, as required in
COA Circular 81-131-A;

e The management should conduct an
evaluation of all motor vehicles to
determine which are still serviceable and
will not cost so much to the agency in terms
of repair; and

e The agency should designate a
mechanic or a person knowledgeable in
motor vehicles to conduct pre and post-
inspection activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Government of
Daet to intensify collections of these long
outstanding  receivables to  generate
additional fund that could be used in the
development projects of this municipality.
Deduct from the payroll of the employces
the amount collectibles and send demand
letters on the receivables from employees
who are already separated from the service.
Likewise, send demand letter to the
recipients of loans for small scale livelihood
projects. Trace from previous records on the
receivables with no particular records or
information of the amount collectibles.
observations.managementAction:

Management complied
with the
recommendations.

observations.recommendation:

various employees separated or dismissed
from the service and refer the matter to the
legal office for legal actions. Likewise, demand for payment from accountable
officers who have unremitted collections
and still in the service paying only through
installment of a little amount.

against them to protect the interest of the
government.
